steps.title: Eliminate symmetric distributions
steps.content:
Mean equals median in symmetric distributions.
  • Choice A — 6, 6, 6, 6, 6, 6, 6, 6, 6: constant 6s - symmetric
  • Choice B — 5, 15, 25, 35, 45, 55, 65, 75, 85: arithmetic sequence - symmetric
Both have mean = median. For mean > median, we need right-skewed data where large values pull the mean upward.

steps.title: Identify exponential growth in choice C
steps.content:
3, 6, 12, 24, 48, 96, 192, 384, 768

This doubles each term, creating severe right skew. The large values at the end (384, 768) pull the mean far above the median.
  • Median = 5th value = 48
  • Sum = 1533
  • Mean = 1533 ÷ 9 = 170.3
Since 170.3 > 48, the mean exceeds the median. That is the correct answer.
